summary: Backend regression tests for expense-to-shift attribution bug fix. All 10 tests pass. Verified: per-cashier expense isolation (A=15,000; B=5,000; no cross-mixing), expense POST stamps shift_id + cashier_id, cash-register-closing report returns 200 with expenses_by_cashier attribution, shift merging fully disabled (closing B leaves A open and unaffected), manager override flow works end-to-end (409 without approval, 409 with wrong password, 200 with valid admin creds), and 2FA dev_code is not leaked in /api/auth/login nor /api/super-admin/pending-2fa-codes responses.
critical_code_review_comments: shifts_routes.py exceeds 1900 lines — consider splitting close_cash_register, get_cash_register_summary, and reports handlers into separate modules for maintainability.

Seed script (seed_expense_attribution_test.py) does not clean auto-created shifts (created by /cash-register/summary auto-open) nor NEWEXP TEST expenses — added session-scoped cleanup fixture in test file to compensate. Consider adding same cleanup inside the seed script itself.

Manager override discrepancy rule requires total_sales > 0 (line 1743). If a shift has zero sales but a huge counted-cash discrepancy vs opening_cash+expenses, no approval is required. Consider also enforcing an absolute-value floor (e.g. any diff > 50,000 IQD) in addition to the 5% rule.
test_credentials: Admin: admin@maestroegp.com/admin123; Owner: owner@maestroegp.com/owner123 (secret_key 271018); Cashiers: expattr-cashier-a@maestroegp.com/test123, expattr-cashier-b@maestroegp.com/test123
seed_data_creation: Uses /app/backend/seed_expense_attribution_test.py (re-run automatically at session start). Test file additionally cleans NEWEXP TEST expenses, EXP-ATTR-TEST-ORDER orders, and any auto-created stray shifts for test cashiers before each session, and re-seeds+injects an order before each discrepancy-override test.
context_for_next_testing_agent: test_expense_attribution.py is the canonical regression test for this accounting-bug fix. It re-seeds automatically. If backend refactors touch routes/shared.py shift_expense_query or routes/shifts_routes.py close/summary handlers, re-run this. Manager-override tests seed a real order via direct MongoDB write to force total_sales>0 (required by the 5% rule at line 1743).
rca of the issue: Original bug: expenses had no shift linkage; attribution was by branch+time-window, causing cross-cashier mixing when multiple cashiers shared a branch/day; each screen used a slightly different query. Fix stamps shift_id + cashier_id on every new expense and uses one canonical shift_expense_query(shift, tenant_id) rule everywhere (routes/shared.py). Legacy expenses without shift_id fall back to branch+business_date+created_by==cashier match. Shift merging on close was removed entirely. Verified end-to-end via 10 pytest cases against the deployed backend.
